evals.prompt: Our marketing site and companion Expo app both feel generic. I want the whole product to feel premium and 'standout' — a signature motion feel across web and mobile. Where do we start?
evals.expected_output: States a one-sentence signature motion thesis, defines a tokenized cross-stack motion system named by intent (durations, easing, springs, depth), maps each surface to the owning implementation skill (gsap / web-three-r3f / r3f-scene-polish for web, expo-motion for native), sets performance and reduced-motion budgets, and sequences the work — rather than hand-animating one component.
assertions: Produces a named signature motion concept/thesis in one sentence before proposing any code
Defines cross-stack motion tokens (durations, easing, springs, depth) named by intent, not raw numeric constants
Routes native/Expo work to expo-motion and web work to gsap / web-three-r3f / r3f-scene-polish rather than implementing every stack itself
Specifies a performance budget and reduced-motion behavior as first-class parts of the system
Does not answer by hand-authoring a single one-off component animation

evals.prompt: We have hardcoded durations and easings scattered across our web and React Native codebases. Design a shared motion-token system we can reuse everywhere.
evals.expected_output: A tokenized architecture covering the token groups (duration, easing, spring, depth, shadow, parallax, blur, glow, quality, reduced-motion) with a single source of truth (one const / CSS variables / Tailwind theme), intent-based naming, and how each stack consumes the tokens (Reanimated vs CSS/GSAP vs R3F).
assertions: Defines tokens grouped by intent (duration/easing/spring/depth/etc.), replacing scattered hardcoded constants
Provides a single source of truth (one const / CSS variables / Tailwind theme) consumed by both web and native
Includes a reduced-motion token group (e.g. fadeOnly / noParallax / noCameraTravel), not only visual tokens
Explains how each stack consumes the tokens and routes stack-specific wiring to the owning skill
Names tokens by intent (short/medium/emphasized/brand) rather than numeric values

evals.prompt: I want one 'signature' card interaction that feels identical whether you're on the R3F-powered web hero or the native iOS app — same physics, same personality. Direct this.
evals.expected_output: Describes the signature card behavior in shared named vocabulary (overshoot/settle, interruption-first, depth), specifies the shared token values (spring, depth, shadow) so the feel matches, then routes the R3F/web implementation to r3f-scene-polish / web-three-r3f and the Reanimated/native implementation to expo-motion, with reduced-motion parity on both.
assertions: Describes the signature interaction in shared named motion vocabulary (e.g. pop-in overshoot, interruption-first) used across both stacks
Specifies shared token values (spring/depth/shadow) so the feel matches on web and native
Routes the web 3D implementation to r3f-scene-polish / web-three-r3f and the native implementation to expo-motion
Requires reduced-motion parity on both platforms so state stays clear when large motion is removed
Keeps the interaction interruptible / velocity-aware rather than fire-and-forget

evals.prompt: Do a repo-wide motion upgrade across all our routes and screens — this is a big migration, not one file. How should we run it?
evals.expected_output: Recognizes repo-wide scope and recommends a dynamic/ultracode workflow that fans out by package/route/screen/scene, classifies stack, finds motion gaps (missing tokens, hardcoded durations, weak choreography, missing reduced-motion, perf risk), has specialists propose fixes, cross-checks with a performance/QA pass, and synthesizes a prioritized plan with a file list and acceptance criteria.
assertions: Recognizes this as repo-wide scope and proposes a dynamic/ultracode workflow rather than editing files one-by-one in the main thread
Fans out by package/route/screen/scene and classifies the stack of each slice
Looks for motion gaps: missing tokens, hardcoded durations, weak choreography, missing reduced-motion, performance risk
Includes a performance/QA cross-check stage and synthesizes a prioritized plan with a file list and acceptance criteria
Routes concrete implementation fixes to the owning per-stack skills
